title: She Almost Threw Away a $1 Million Lottery Ticket
script: Nearly trashed $1 million? Melissa Johnson, a woman from Hendersonville, North Carolina, bought a $10 Loteria Multiplier scratch-off from Rock Convenience on Chimney Rock Road. She scratched it. Thought it was a loser. And was about to throw it in the trash. But something stopped her — she pulled out her phone and scanned it with the North Carolina Education Lottery app. The app said: file a claim. A worker told her she must've won something. She scanned it again. One million dollars. She had been seconds away from tossing $1 million into the garbage. Officials say Melissa plans to use the money wisely, though they didn't specify what that means. The ticket nearly died in a trash can in Hendersonville. So: how many winning tickets die in trash cans in Hendersonville?
